name: Flip Wilson
bio: Flip Wilson was an American comedian and actor who became the first African American to host a successful network television variety show, The Flip Wilson Show, which ran from 1970 to 1974. He was celebrated for his story-driven stand-up, including the routine Christopher Columbus, and for character creations such as Geraldine, and was named TVs first black superstar by Time magazine.

timeline.ev: First appearance on The Tonight Show, recommended by Redd Foxx
Columbus routine on the album Cowboys and Colored People draws attention
The Flip Wilson Show premieres on NBC
Wins a Grammy for The Devil Made Me Buy This Dress
Featured on the cover of Time as TVs first black superstar

accolades: Grammy Award for The Devil Made Me Buy This Dress (1971)
Golden Globe Award for The Flip Wilson Show
First African American to host a successful TV variety show

specials.t: The Devil Made Me Buy This Dress
